name: wireshark-common
title: wireshark-common
description:
network traffic analyzer - common files
Wireshark is a network "sniffer" - a tool that captures and analyzes
packets off the wire. Wireshark can decode too many protocols to list
here.
.
This package provides files common to both wireshark and tshark
(the console version).

commands.name: captype
commands.description: Prints the types of capture files

commands.name: editcap
commands.description: Edit and/or translate the format of capture files

commands.name: mergecap
commands.description: Merges two or more capture files into one

commands.name: mmdbresolve
commands.description: Read IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and print their IP geolocation information.

commands.name: rawshark
commands.description: Dump and analyze raw pcap data

commands.name: reordercap
commands.description: Reorder input file by timestamp into output file

commands.name: text2pcap
commands.description: Generate a capture file from an ASCII hex dump of packets

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use capinfos because network traffic analyzer - common files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use capinfos as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `capinfos  [  -a ] [ -A ] [ -b ] [ -B ] [ -c ] [ -C ] [ -d ] [ -D ] [ -e ] [ -E ] [ -F ] [ -h ]` as documented upstream for capinfos.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `capinfos -h|--help` as documented upstream for capinfos.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `capinfos -v|--version` as documented upstream for capinfos.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `capinfos -h` as documented upstream for capinfos.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair capinfos with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `capinfos --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: capinfos - Prints information about capture files
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install wireshark. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `capinfos  [  -a ] [ -A ] [ -b ] [ -B ] [ -c ] [ -C ] [ -d ] [ -D ] [ -e ] [ -E ] [ -F ] [ -h ]`. Further invocations from upstream: `capinfos -h|--help`; `capinfos -v|--version`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: network traffic analyzer - common files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: capinfos - Prints information about capture files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install wireshark
